_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Stale origin/main ref breaks make diff-coverage in worktrees

steps.description: Add `git fetch . main:refs/remotes/origin/main` to executor/setup_worktree.sh immediately after `git worktree add`. Also add the same one-liner as a defensive safeguard at the top of the `diff-coverage` Makefile target. Both must be idempotent / fast / no-op when already in sync.
Per-agent review of S01: idempotency, no GitHub-flow regression, log line presence (optional but nice).

Author tests/integration/test_setup_worktree_origin_main_sync.py — reproduction test (stale origin/main → setup_worktree → origin/main = main) + AC3 verification (diff-coverage gives correct file list).
Per-agent review of S03 tests: semantic assertions, both ACs covered, no live-DB usage.
Cross-agent final review of S01..S04: scope discipline, no Makefile target regressions for other gates.
QV: Diff coverage (this very gate is what the fix improves; it MUST pass cleanly here)
Self-assessment via iw-item-analyze — focus on whether S12 (diff-coverage) of this very CR ran clean (it should, because the fix is now in effect for this worktree).
